title: rosa glauca subsp. reuteri
intro: Rosa glauca subsp. reuteri, also known as the rose of Sharon or alder-leaved rose, is a plant species in the rose family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Rosa glauca subsp. reuteri has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in cooking and as a natural dye.
